critical.module_dag: types -> core -> context -> view -> operations -> tools

packages.types: Value types (Contract, Context, EdgeId) - no internal deps
packages.core: Cascade graph, Node, NodeState with transition rules
packages.view: Presentation layer for agent task views
packages.operations: Split, Remove, Rework operations with base class
packages.storage: JSON persistence with fcntl file locking + EventStore

context.summary: Cascade is a DAG-based multi-agent task scheduler. 6 packages: types (value types), core (Cascade graph, Node, NodeState), context (propagation, cancellation), view (agent-facing presentation), operations (split/remove/rework), storage (JSON persistence with file locking). Event sourcing via append-only JSONL log. 11 tools expose the API. Key patterns: mandatory contracts on every edge, computed readiness (never cached), forward-only rework (new corrective nodes, never mutate parent), critical-path scheduling via downstream depth, Go-style cancellation tokens, 3-tier context propagation (critical/summary/artifacts).
CLAUDE.md created using upstream audit context
README.md updated using upstream audit context

Verified CLAUDE.md and README.md are consistent and accurate. Both correctly document: module DAG (types->core->context->view->operations->tools), all 11 tools, state machine (PENDING->READY->ACTIVE->COMPLETED plus release and cancel/fail transitions), 7 design patterns, 8 key types, and 6 packages. README Quick Start imports match actual __init__.py exports. docs/usage.md link is valid. No issues found.
